Scenario.overview: Communications Industry Simple The ShadowRay group targets communications firms using a straightforward yet effective approach. They exploit public-facing applications to gain initial access, execute malicious payloads, and impact operations through data encryption.
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Command and Scripting Interpreter, Data Encrypted for Impact

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121; Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: The attackers exploit CVE-2023-48022 in the Ray AI framework exposed on the internet, gaining unauthorized access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 160; Name: Exploit Script-Based APIs
stage.description: After gaining access, the attackers utilize command-line interfaces or scripting interpreters to run malicious scripts and commands.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 609; Name: Cellular Traffic Intercept
stage.description: The attackers encrypt critical files on the file system, disrupting services and demanding a ransom for decryption keys.

Scenario.summary: The ShadowRay group employs a direct and potent strategy against communications firms, exploiting known vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to infiltrate networks, executing malicious payloads, and ultimately impacting operations through data encryption, aiming for financial gain and disruption.
